data.title: Iran to Repatriate Bodies of 84 Naval Officers Killed in U.S. Submarine Attack
data.description: Iran is set to repatriate the bodies of 84 naval officers who were killed in a U.S. submarine attack, according to Jin10. The incident has heightened tensions between the two nations, with Iran's foreign embassy sources confirming the return of the deceased officers. The attack underscores ongoing geopolitical frictions in the region, as both countries navigate complex diplomatic relations. The repatriation process is expected to proceed in the coming days, marking a somber moment for the families and the nation.

data.title: Polymarket's first Shanghai weather forecast: Nearly 100% probability of a high of 13°C, up 55% intraday.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of Polymarket's prediction of a high of 13 degrees Celsius in Shanghai on March 13th has risen to 99%, an increase of 55% within the day. Previously, Polymarket launched weather forecasts for Shanghai and Hong Kong yesterday; however, due to Wunderground's inability to provide historical weather data for Hong Kong, the Hong Kong-related forecasts have been removed today. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the forecast market, anticipating changes before pricing.

data.title: UK Chancellor Invites Competition Authority to Fuel Station Operators Meeting
data.description: UK Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rachel Reeves, has extended an invitation to the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) to participate in a meeting with fuel station operators. According to Jin10, this move aims to address concerns regarding fuel pricing and market competition. The meeting is expected to facilitate discussions on ensuring fair pricing practices and enhancing transparency within the fuel retail sector. The involvement of the CMA underscores the government's commitment to maintaining competitive market conditions and protecting consumer interests.

data.title: New York's Job Market for Recent Graduates Faces Challenges
data.description: The job market for recent college graduates in New York is experiencing a downturn, affecting what was once a promising aspect of the city's economy. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges faced by new graduates as they enter the workforce. The decline in job opportunities is contributing to broader economic concerns in the region. As the situation unfolds, stakeholders are closely monitoring the impact on the city's economic landscape.

data.title: The Dubai TOKEN 2049 Summit is tentatively scheduled to be postponed to April 2027.
data.description: According to market sources, the organizers of the Dubai TOKEN2049 Summit plan to postpone the event to April 21-22, 2027. The conference was originally scheduled to be held from April 29 to 30, 2026, but the recent conflict in the Middle East has spread to Dubai.

data.title: Binance to Expand Monitoring Tag to Include Additional Tokens in March 2026
data.description:
Binance, a leading centralized exchange (CEX), has announced plans to extend its monitoring tag to include several additional tokens on March 13, 2026. According to BWEnews, the tokens to be included are ATA, A2Z, FIO, GTC, NTRN, PHB, QI, and RDNT. This move is part of Binance's ongoing efforts to enhance its monitoring capabilities and ensure compliance with regulatory standards.

The market capitalization for these tokens varies, with A2Z, FIO, and RDNT each having a market cap of $8 million. GTC and NTRN both have a market cap of $10 million, while QI leads with $14 million. PHB and ATA also have a market cap of $10 million and $12 million, respectively.

This development is expected to impact the trading and monitoring processes on the platform, as Binance continues to adapt to the evolving cryptocurrency landscape. The inclusion of these tokens under the monitoring tag signifies Binance's commitment to maintaining a secure and transparent trading environment for its users.

data.title: A whale bought 86,259 HYPE tokens 7 hours ago, worth $3.27 million.

data.title: Binance has added ATA, A2Z, FIO, GTC, NTRN, PHB, QI, and RDNT to its monitoring tags.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Binance added ATA, A2Z, FIO, GTC, NTRN, PHB, QI, and RDNT to its monitoring tags on March 13, 2026.

data.title: European Stocks Face Second Weekly Decline Amid Iran Conflict Concerns
data.description:
European stock markets are poised for a second consecutive weekly decline as the ongoing conflict in Iran raises concerns about rising inflation and slowing economic growth. According to Jin10, worries about private credit are also quietly escalating. Mining stocks are leading the decline, with bank stocks also underperforming.

Since the beginning of the year, European stocks have outperformed U.S. stocks, driven by optimistic expectations of increased fiscal spending and lower interest rates. However, this trend has been hampered in March due to fears that the Iran conflict could lead to a stagflation shock. Data from EPFR Global, cited by Bank of America, shows that European equity funds experienced a $200 million outflow in the week ending Wednesday, marking the first net outflow in six weeks.

Rhynhardt Roodt, Chief Investment Officer of Equities at Ninety One, stated, "The market is pricing in the risk of a prolonged conflict, which will have a greater impact on Europe than the U.S. If oil prices remain above $100, current inflation expectations may still be underestimated; the longer the conflict drags on, the more concerned I become about the stock market."

data.title: India Plans Economic Stability Fund to Tackle Global Challenges
data.description: India's Finance Minister has announced plans to establish an Economic Stability Fund aimed at providing fiscal space to address global headwinds, including recent crises. According to Jin10, this initiative is part of India's broader strategy to enhance its economic resilience amid ongoing global uncertainties. The fund is expected to offer a financial buffer, enabling the country to navigate economic challenges more effectively. This move reflects India's proactive approach to safeguarding its economy against potential disruptions in the global market.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin may retest $100,000; macroeconomic indicators show bullish signals.
data.description: A macroeconomic model combining the yields of 10-year US Treasury bonds and 10-year Chinese government bonds (US10Y×CN10Y) recently generated an "extremely accurate" bullish crossover signal, potentially providing guidance for the next Bitcoin rally. Historical data shows that this indicator issued similar signals before the bull markets of 2013, 2017, 2020–2021, and 2023, corresponding to Bitcoin gains of approximately 8700%, 1900%, 600%, and over 350%, respectively. Furthermore, on-chain data shows that whale addresses (wallets holding 1000–10000 BTC) have re-entered a accumulating phase during the recent price correction, similar to behavior patterns seen near market bottoms in previous instances. Analysts believe that if Bitcoin stabilizes and rebounds from near its 200-week moving average, the price may test $100,000 around August. However, if it fails to break through the key resistance level of $78,000, there remains a risk of a "bull trap." (Cointelegraph)

data.title: South Korean Won Briefly Surpasses 1,500 Against U.S. Dollar
data.description: The exchange rate between the South Korean won and the U.S. dollar briefly exceeded 1,500 at approximately 8:24 a.m. UTC on March 13. According to NS3.AI, this marks the first occurrence of the rate reaching this level since March 4.

data.title: EU Commission Advises Against Costly Gas Storage Replenishment
data.description: The European Commission has advised the EU Gas and Oil Coordination Group against replenishing gas storage facilities at any cost. According to Jin10, the Commission emphasized the importance of balancing energy security with financial prudence. This guidance comes amid ongoing discussions about energy strategies and the need to ensure stable supply without incurring excessive expenses. The EU continues to navigate the complexities of energy management, seeking to maintain a steady supply while avoiding unnecessary financial burdens.

data.title: China Approves Brain Implant for Partially Paralyzed Adults
data.description: China's drug regulator has granted marketing approval to Neuracle Technology for a brain implant designed for adult patients experiencing partial paralysis. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this development in the medical technology sector. The approval marks a significant step forward in the treatment options available for individuals with paralysis, potentially improving their quality of life. Neuracle Technology's innovation is expected to contribute to advancements in neurological treatments and rehabilitation.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 72,000 USDT with a 3.22% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 13, 2026, 08:41 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 72,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 72,005.851563 USDT, with a narrowed 3.22% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: EU Calls for Assessment of IEA's Decision Impact on Supply Security
data.description: The European Union has urged an evaluation of the International Energy Agency's recent decision and its potential impact on supply security in the medium term. According to Jin10, the EU's call comes in response to concerns about the stability and reliability of energy supplies following the IEA's actions. The EU emphasizes the importance of understanding the implications of these decisions to ensure energy security and stability across member states. This assessment is seen as crucial for maintaining a balanced and secure energy supply chain in the region.

data.title: Michael B. Jordan Leads Oscar Best Actor Predictions
data.description: Michael B. Jordan has emerged as the frontrunner in the Oscar Best Actor prediction event, boasting a 58% probability of winning. According to NS3.AI, this development marks a significant shift in the market's previous rankings, where Timothee Chalamet's chances have decreased to 32%.

data.title: Shapoorji Pallonji Group Seeks New Overseas Investors
data.description: Shapoorji Pallonji Group is in talks with potential new overseas investors to expand its funding sources. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the group's efforts to diversify its financial backing. The discussions aim to strengthen the company's financial position by attracting international investment. This move is part of a broader strategy to enhance the group's global presence and financial stability.

data.title: Serbia Acquires Supersonic Missiles from China in Defense Upgrade
data.description: Serbia has purchased supersonic, air-to-surface missiles from China as part of a $17 billion defense enhancement, according to President Aleksandar Vucic. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move aimed at bolstering Serbia's military capabilities. This acquisition is part of a broader effort to modernize the country's defense systems, reflecting Serbia's commitment to strengthening its national security infrastructure. The deal underscores the growing defense ties between Serbia and China, as Serbia continues to invest heavily in its military advancements.
